commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=02c1140c4aeab971a49c43b9bc3bdeb914b1879f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR exports and documents the isolated-cron owner-only allowlist helper, keeps unattended owner-only grants limited to `cron`, and adds focused unit coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for the main PR behavior: this documents and tests an existing helper boundary rather than fixing a runtime bug. The small wording issue is source-checkable by comparing the new comment with `src/agents/tool-policy.ts`.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane should run while the contributor still needs to update PR-body proof fields; maintainers can review after the proof gate and minor wording issue are resolved.

target.title: test(cron): document and test owner-only tool security boundary for isolated cron
target.body:
## Summary

Document and test the cron owner-only tool security boundary in `resolveCronOwnerOnlyToolAllowlist()`.

Isolated cron runs are executed with `senderIsOwner: false`. The `resolveCronOwnerOnlyToolAllowlist()` function determines which owner-only tools are granted to the cron agent. Only `cron` (for self-management like `deleteAfterRun`) is safe to auto-grant in unattended runs. Control-plane tools (`gateway`, `nodes`) must not be auto-granted.

### Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `run-executor.ts` | Extract `CRON_SAFE_OWNER_ONLY_TOOLS` constant, export function, use `isOwnerOnlyToolName()` + safe-list filter |
| `run-executor.owner-only-allowlist.test.ts` | 6 test cases covering the security boundary |

### What this does NOT fix

Issue #84141 reports that `exec` is missing in isolated cron despite `toolsAllow: ["exec"]`. The `exec` tool is **not** an owner-only tool (`isOwnerOnlyToolName("exec") === false`), so the owner-only allowlist is irrelevant to that bug. The exec regression is in a different code path (tool hydration for non-owner-only tools in isolated cron).

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.owner-only-allowlist.test.ts
 ✓ unit-fast (6 tests)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed (6)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: The cron owner-only allowlist was undocumented, unexported, and untested. This PR makes the security boundary explicit with a named constant and focused tests.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, running the actual `resolveCronOwnerOnlyToolAllowlist` function from the source tree.

### Owner-only tool classification (tool-policy.ts)

```
cron:    isOwnerOnly=true   ← only this is cron-safe
gateway: isOwnerOnly=true   ← control-plane, NOT cron-safe
nodes:   isOwnerOnly=true   ← exec-capable, NOT cron-safe
exec:    isOwnerOnly=false  ← NOT owner-only at all (#84141 is unrelated)
```

### Security boundary proof

```
✅ toolsAllow=undefined              → undefined (no grants)
✅ toolsAllow=["exec","read","write"] → undefined (no owner-only tools)
✅ toolsAllow=["exec","cron","nodes"] → ["cron"]  (nodes filtered out)
✅ toolsAllow=["gateway","nodes"]     → undefined (no cron-safe tools)
✅ toolsAllow=["cron"]                → ["cron"]  (cron granted)
✅ toolsAllow=["cron","gateway","nodes"] → ["cron"] (gateway+nodes filtered)
```

6/6 cases pass. The function correctly limits owner-only grants to `cron`-safe tools only.

- **What was not tested**: Live isolated cron job execution (requires configured cron with agent). The function-level proof validates the security boundary. Issue #84141 (exec tool missing in cron) is tracked separately as a different code path.
target.author: jerry-xin

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
